description: Publish request whose signature.key_id DID portion is not did:web (even though agent_id is did:web). RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4 (DID method scope table) and §5.11 require the DID portion of key_id to be did:web in v0.0.1. The DID portion MUST also equal agent_id (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11 step 2; see also pub-006 for the equality check). When agent_id and key_id DIDs differ in method, the registry MUST reject — typically with key_not_authorized after the equality check fires (because did:web:agent.example != did:key:z6Mk...).

body.title: did:key key_id with did:web agent_id — non-conformant

expected.rationale: RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11 step 2 requires the DID portion of key_id to equal agent_id. The two DIDs here differ in both the method (web vs key) and the identifier, so the equality check fails and the registry returns key_not_authorized before any DID resolution is attempted.

related: pub-006 (same registry behavior for same-method mismatched DIDs); pub-008 (non-did:web agent_id with matching key_id).
notes: This fixture targets the scope-table row for signature.key_id. A registry that strips the equality check and relies only on key resolution would still reject (because did:key resolution is out of v0.0.1 scope and the §5.11 step 3 fetch would fail), but the spec-preferred response is the equality-check rejection at step 2.
